differentials.name: Biloma formation (11%) within 1st year after transplantation
differentials.raw: Biloma formation (11%) within 1st year after transplantation

context: ◊  2nd most common cause of liver dysfunction after rejection Time of onset:   within first 3 months Stricture and leak = the most common biliary complications Cause:   iatrogenic trauma → ischemia + scar formation (b)  nonanastomotic (intrahepatic) stricture Cause:   hepatic arterial thrombosis / stenosis (in 50%), prolonged preservation time, bacterial / viral cholangitis, rejection, recurrent primary sclerosing cholangitis, cholangiocarcinoma, kinking of redundant CBD, sphincter of Oddi dysfunction (c)  tension mucocele of allograft cystic duct remnant Cause:   ligation of cystic duct proxi
